MINI MINUTES FOR JAN-18-2018 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 503

ASBESTOS MOTION CALENDAR ON JAN-18-2018 IN 503, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR PREFERENCE IS GRANTED PURSUANT TO C.C.P. 36(A). TRIAL DATE IS MAY 7, 2018 AT 11:15AM IN DEPARTMENT 503. LAST DAY PURSUANT TO C.C.P. 36(F) IS MAY 18, 2018. THE LAST DAY FOR HEARING SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION MOTIONS IS MAY 2, 2018. SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION MOTIONS SHALL BE BROUGHT ON REGULAR NOTICE PURSUANT TO THE RELEVANT PROVISIONS OF THE C.C.P., UNLESS THE PARTIES STIPULATE OTHERWISE. SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE IN ROOM 603 AT 9:30A.M. ON MAY 3, 2018. FURTHER CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE ON MARCH 8, 2018 AT 1:30P.M. THERE WILL BE A MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE WITH THE COURT PURSUANT TO CRC, RULE 3.1380 AND SAN FRANCISCO LOCAL RULES OF COURT, RULES 5.0 AND 20.2 ON MAY 7, 2018 AT 1:30P.M. IN DEPARTMENT 503. THE PARTIES SHALL COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULES 5.0 AND 20.2 AND PROVIDE A CONFIDENTIAL SETTLEMENT STATEMENT BY MAY 3, 2018 AT 2:00P.M. ORDER SIGNED IN CHAMBERS. JUDGE: CYNTHIA MING-MEI LEE, MATTER NOT REPORTED

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE ON MAR-08-2018 AT 1:30 PM, DEPT. 503 IS HELD. DESIGNATED DEFENSE COUNSEL STATES THAT DEFENDANTS JUST RECEIVED THE FOLLOWING DATES FOR PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION; 4/3, 4/4, AND 4/5. THE COURT AND COUNSEL CONFER REGARDING THE POSSIBILITY OF EARLIER DATES FOR THE PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION, SHORTENING TIME FOR MSJ'S, AND THE PENDING TRIAL DATE OF 5/7/18. AT THE CONCLUSION OF DISCUSSIONS, THE COURT CONTINUED THE CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE TO MAR-15-2018 AT 1:30 PM IN DEPT. 503. COUNSEL ARE TO MEET AND CONFER REGARDING POSSIBLE EARLIER DATES FOR THE PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION AND SHORTENING TIME FOR MOTIONS FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. JUDGE: LYNN O'MALLEY TAYLOR, MATTER NOT REPORTED. Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff (Added To Calendar For Issuance Of Tentative Ruling Only.)
Off Calendar Jan-18-2018 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff
Off Calendar Jan-18-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Andrew Chew of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff and moving party GERALD SMITH and JOSEPHINE SMITH
Alexander J. Alpert of WFBM, LLP (415-781-7072), appearing for defendant Thomas Dee Engineering Company
Megan Van Arsdale of FOLEY & MANSFIELD (510-590-9500), appearing for defendant Republic Supply Company
Constance Fraenkel of SPANOS | PRZETAK (510-250-0200), Designated Defense Counsel

RULING:
Matter not argued and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's motion for preference is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a). No opposition filed. Trial date is May 7, 2018 at 11:15am in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is May 18, 2018. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is May 2, 2018. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Plaintiff to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than March 23, 2018. Responses to the demand are due no later than April 6, 2018. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands stating their best and final offers. These confidential demands must be submitted to Pang Ly by April 30, 2018 at 2:00p.m. Settlement conference in room 603 at 9:30a.m. on May 3, 2018. Fact discovery cut-off date is April 20, 2018. Expert discovery cut-off date is May 4, 2018. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than February 16, 2018. If plaintiff submits documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, he must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents. No later than February 1, 2018 plaintiff must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiff's claims. No later than February 1, 2018 defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Further case management conference on March 8, 2018 at 1:30p.m. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two day prior to take the CMC off calendar. There will be a mandatory settlement conference with the Court pursuant to CRC, Rule 3.1380 and San Francisco Local Rules of Court, Rules 5.0 and 20.2 on May 7, 2018 at 1:30p.m. in Department 503. The parties shall comply with Local Rules 5.0 and 20.2 and provide a confidential settlement statement by May 3, 2018 at 2:00p.m. Order signed in chambers. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Matter not reported. (503/CML)
